children.text: Old Hill is one of Westport’s quieter residential enclaves, known for a calm streetscape and a more tucked-away feel than the downtown/Saugatuck corridor. It’s the kind of neighborhood where the setting itself—trees, mature landscaping, and a steady cadence—does a lot of the work.

Real estate is predominantly single-family, and the appeal often comes down to balance: you get Westport’s location and lifestyle, but your day-to-day at home feels quiet and residential. In my experience, Old Hill can be a strong fit for clients who want Westport without wanting to feel “in the middle of it.”

From Old Hill, Westport’s core amenities are still close—Main Street dining and shopping, Metro-North access at Westport station on the New Haven Line, and shoreline time at Compo Beach. Old Hill is best for buyers who want a private-feeling neighborhood with Westport’s village and coastal lifestyle within easy reach.

highlights: A quiet Westport enclave with an established, residential feel
Predominantly single-family homes with mature landscaping and character
Easy access to downtown Westport and Main Street dining/shopping
Westport Metro-North (New Haven Line) within reach for commuting
Compo Beach Park nearby for shoreline recreation
name: Old Hill
overview: Old Hill is a quiet Westport enclave with an established, residential feel and a strong sense of neighborhood character. Homes are primarily single-family, and the pace is calm—close enough to downtown for convenience, but removed enough to feel private. It’s a great fit for buyers who want Westport with a more tucked-away home base while keeping Main Street, the train, and the shoreline within easy reach.
